title: Detect Fully Synced Voice Memos in the iCloud Directory
creator: [You]

userStory.persona: a user who just recorded a voice memo on my iPhone
userStory.want: Utterd to detect the new audio file once it has fully synced to my Mac
userStory.soThat: processing begins automatically without me taking any action

context: The iCloud Voice Memos sync directory is located at the path confirmed during the spike. Files arrive as .m4a audio. iCloud may sync files progressively, meaning a file could appear before its audio data is fully available. The detection method for sync completion was determined during the spike.

acceptanceCriteria.given: Utterd is running and monitoring the Voice Memos sync directory
acceptanceCriteria.when: a new .m4a file is added to the directory and fully synced
acceptanceCriteria.then: the watcher emits the complete file path for downstream processing

acceptanceCriteria.given: Utterd is running and monitoring the Voice Memos sync directory
acceptanceCriteria.when: a new file appears that is not .m4a (such as .txt, .jpg, or .plist)
acceptanceCriteria.then: the watcher ignores the file and does not emit it for processing

acceptanceCriteria.given: Utterd is running and monitoring the Voice Memos sync directory
acceptanceCriteria.when: a .m4a file appears but has not finished syncing
acceptanceCriteria.then: the watcher waits until the file is fully synced before emitting it

acceptanceCriteria.given: the Mac has been asleep and wakes up
acceptanceCriteria.when: new .m4a files synced to the directory during sleep
acceptanceCriteria.then: the watcher detects and emits each new file after wake

acceptanceCriteria.given: Utterd starts for the first time
acceptanceCriteria.when: the sync directory already contains previously recorded .m4a files
acceptanceCriteria.then: the watcher does not emit those existing files — only files arriving after startup are processed

acceptanceCriteria.when: two voice memos are recorded in quick succession and both sync within seconds of each other
acceptanceCriteria.then: both files are detected and emitted in the order they completed syncing

acceptanceCriteria.when: the sync directory does not exist (iCloud is not configured or Voice Memos has never been used)
acceptanceCriteria.then: the watcher logs a descriptive error including the expected directory path and does not crash

outOfScope: Persisting which files have been seen (that's the state tracker in Story 3), transcribing audio, any UI feedback. This story produces file path events and nothing more.
